_comment: Example MCP server config for Claude Code. Add this entry to your existing mcp_servers.json (or equivalent). The trawl-mcp server speaks stdio, so Claude Code will spawn it when the fetch_page tool is invoked. Requires trawl to be pip-installed (with its console script) in the Python environment on PATH.
_notes: If you haven't installed the `trawl-mcp` console script on PATH, use one of: (a) `{"command": "mamba", "args": ["run", "-n", "trawl", "python", "-m", "trawl_mcp"]}` if you installed trawl into a mamba env named `trawl`, or (b) `{"command": "/absolute/path/to/python", "args": ["-m", "trawl_mcp"]}` pointing at a venv's interpreter.

The `env` block lets you point at a different embedding server without touching trawl's source. All env vars follow the TRAWL_{COMPONENT}_{PROPERTY} convention — see README.md and .env.example for the full list.
Cloudflare-protected sites (Stack Overflow, etc.) take ~10-20s per fetch because playwright-stealth has to solve the JS challenge. Set a generous tool timeout on the client side.
